condition.selected: Scale of 5 — it shows more detail.

steps.text: Scale of 5 works, but look at 90. Count by 5s: you need 19 tick marks to reach it. Scale of 10 only needs 10. Which is easier to read?

condition.selected: Scale of 2 — it works for any numbers.

steps.text: The biggest number is 90. Scale of 2 means counting 0, 2, 4, 6, all the way to 90. That's 46 marks on the axis. Way too crowded. Try a bigger scale.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 10. Steps Walked data displayed. Ana: 30, Ben: 70, Cara: 90, Dan: 50. Axis shows 0, 10, 20...100 with 11 tick marks.

steps.text: Let me show you. The biggest number is 90. All values end in 0, so they're multiples of 10. With Scale of 10, every bar lands exactly on a line: 30 at 30, 50 at 50, 70 at 70, 90 at 90. The axis only needs 10 marks to reach 90. Clean and easy to read. For big numbers that are multiples of 10, Scale of 10 is the best choice.
